events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: worker tied off to a guardrail top rail, which is not rated for personal fall arrest anchorage. OSHA 1926.502 covers guardrail systems and fall protection. Guardrails are designed to withstand 200 lbs force, while PFAS anchors must support 5,000 lbs per worker (or engineered 2:1 safety factor). This is non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical. Work stage: in-progress. Component conditions: non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 50? The task is in progress. Let's estimate maybe 40. Value: 0. Confidence high. Findings: tie-off to guardrail not rated for PFAS. Actions: stop work, move to engineered anchor or use mobile anchor/retractable, retrain. Rationale: guardrail strength (200 lb) vs anchorage (5000 lb). References: OSHA 1926.502.

    "Relocate the worker to the engineered anchor or provide a compliant mobile anchor point/SRL",

OSHA 1926.502 guardrails are designed to resist about 200 lb of outward/downward force, while PFAS anchors must support 5,000 lb per worker (or meet engineered 2:1 factor criteria). The short task duration and distance to the engineered anchor do not override the anchorage requirement.",

    "OSHA 1926.502(d) - Personal fall arrest systems anchorage strength"
  ]
